ab: ka qaqunuan a nia umaq i tjuabar, ka magaku anga, patjavat a sema taihuk. imazamaza i hici a sikac.
ch: 小時候我們家在土坂，上學以後，就搬到了台北，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in tjuabar. After I started school, we moved to Taipei and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: ka temakesi anga ta daigaku, na peniliq a ken tua ita pakata na kacalisian bunka a kituluan, pualang anga za kacalisian tu izua maretimaljimalji na talidu a bunka i taiwan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: manasika, tucu a cavilj nu pakilavay tua kaljazalangezangan, uli vaik amen kata u kaka uqaljay sema qinaljan, kivadaq ta ramaljemaljeng, saljinga tu kemeljang tua nimitja zuku a bunka.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: aza ramaljeng alu puluq anga cavilj, ljakua nu tja pacunan matu tjaukaramaljeng.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: u kivadaqan timadju macaqu tua pida kai. tauliq ravac nu lukai timadju ta pinaiwanan, saigu a lukai ta na muqami a kai tauta.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的排灣語說得很流利，也會說日本話和阿美語。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Paiwan, as well as Japanese and Pangcah.

ab: manu ka qaqunuan timadju na kitulu ta pinaiwanan, a cekelj amizuku, sa za sengsengan nimadju makakeljan kata tjuluvu a ripung caucau, avansika liaw kinasiagu a kai.
ch: 原來他小時候就學排灣語，太太是阿美族人，而他工作上認識許多日本人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Paiwan languages when he was a child, his wife is an Pangcah, and he knows many Japenese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: ka kitjaucikel amen, aza ramaljeng na tjemaucikel tu liaw pakata qemaljup a kakudan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: kumalji ika men maqati a sepualang kiselangda, ljakua na emaya men ta itua lima dingwa a remuing, maqati mumalj kigalju a kilangeda.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: ka qaqunuan ika pualang tua pasa niaken zuku a bunka, pakata kacalisian angata ken izua keri a siaqan.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: ka tjaivililj liaw anga pinualang, u kinemenemen tiaken paiwanzuku a caucau, na pakisumalji angata, pacengceng si ilasian uta.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，我覺得自己身為排灣族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a paiwan is unique and something to be proud of.
